zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,於1308年出生在蘇格蘭費伏時，她的父親Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots34歲，母親Mistresses47歲。她於1334年11月10日與Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto在英國蘇格蘭吉德布萊次爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1364年3月7日在她的家鄉享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,于1308年出生在苏格兰費伏时，她的父亲Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots34岁，母亲Mistresses47岁。她于1334年11月10日与Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto在英国苏格兰吉德布萊次爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1364年3月7日在她的家乡享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Glen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (central Scotland): habitational name from Glen in Traquair (Peebleshire). The placename is derived from Gaelic gleann ‘glen, valley’.
English: habitational name from a place so called in Leicestershire, so named from an Old English word glean ‘glen, valley’ (from Celtic glinn).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish names.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, was born in 1308, in Fife, Scotland, her father, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, was 34 and her mother, Mistresses, was 47. She married Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto on 10 November 1334, in Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 7 March 1364, in her hometown, at the age of 56.

fr.Balmuto Castle, Fife, Scotland: Castle, Jackson, Dakota du Sud, États-Unis
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland, est née en 1308, à Fife, Écosse, son père, Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Mistresses, avait 47 ans. Elle a épousé Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto le 10 novembre 1334, à Kirkcudbrightshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 7 mars 1364, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans.

Sie hat am 10. November 1334 in Kirkcudbrightshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 7. ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret De Glen Countess of Balmuto, Princess of Scotland,(이)가 1308년 스컷랜드 피프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mistresses(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 1334년 11월 10일 영국 스코틀랜드 커크쿠드브라이츠히르에서 Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1364년 3월 7일 그녀의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert De La Glenn, Earl of Balmuto 10 ноября 1334 г. в местности кирккудбраицхир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 7 марта 1364 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 56 лет.
